review_title: Extremely simple tool to implement into any business that wants to coach employees on phone calls.
description: Able to easily and effortlessly record all calls that are taken inbound and made outbound. Can listen to those calls, review them, make comments on them and share them with people both inside and outside my company.
author: Christopher F.
pros: It easily allows me to pick any call (inbound or outbound) that I made on any day to listen, comment, critique and send to anyone inside or outside of my company to review. We use this on a daily basis to coach our team on phone techniques. It makes our team feel way more comfortable than having management listen in on live calls as they dial!
cons: I am not sure if this is necessarily a big deal, but we wish we were notified every time enhancements were made to the software. Sometimes changes will be made without us knowing what is new or how to use the new features. Again, not a big deal and not something we are going to lose any sleep over. Execvision is well worth it!

review_title: I used Execvision to record calls I have with Sales customers
description: I was able to review some calls I had and analyze where the went wrong/right.
author: Gavin G.
pros: For the most part Execvision is easy to use as it tied into another sales tool we had which made it easy to record calls.
cons: Although it was easy to use, many times my calls would not record properly or would be lost somewhere which made it difficult to track actual results from my calls. calls that I had that were very good would often not be found and therefore could not share with my managers.

review_title: This tool is crap
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It does provide a fairly simple method to record calls. I can't take that away despite how terrible and entitled the people who work for them are.
cons: They are run by a con-man who swoops in and preaches Inside Sales techniques from the 90s and charges a ransom. The platform owns every call that is recorded through it so from time to time you'll just receive email notifications that some random person is listening to your call and roasting you in front of a crowd - no consent. The 'support team' is also super condescending and acts like they are the best salespeople on earth without understanding the difference in transactional vs enterprise sales.

review_title: It is super user friendly and allows for real time adjustments, which in sales is everything.
author: TAZIA C.
pros: The best thing about the tool is it is very user friendly and did not take much time on my part to learn how to use which makes it easier to use since I do not have to waste time trying to learn the tool. ExecVision has been a major pillar in my coaching sessions with my manager and I can adjust the way I am pitching our product to potential prospects. If I feel like a call I had went bad I can collaborate with my management team or other colleagues and get there feedback on what went well and what I can improve on. All in all the next time I pick up the phone I am better than I was before because I am allowed to adjust in real time which has helped me book more appointments and be better on the phone. It is a great product and I love using it!
cons: Honestly I do not have any cons, Excevision does exactly what it is meant to do and I have had nothing but success from it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: ExecVision - Truly Amazing
description: Never heard of it before working at my current job. Great new Software that should be applied to all new trainee SDRs and BDRs. Also, great for salesperson(s) all around.
author: Brandon M.
pros: One of the best coaching tools out there. Really great for the beginners and the experts who are trying to refine their pitch - it is great as well.
cons: Would like to have more calls camps integrated into having your calls reviewed.

review_title: Competitive pricing, feature can be improved to match competitors
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: out of box reporting and analytics features. intuitive for users who have had experience in such reporting tools
cons: features could be improved to provide more deductive results, more statistical results, for deeper insights. Agree this could be challenging. such supports in the major brands are also clunky today. so an opportunity for execvision to improve and overtake.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Execvision at a Glance
author: Ian B.
pros: I like color coding different call segments, the ability to rate each call, and give/get feedback
cons: The amount fo time it takes for the integration with salesforce to pull the call over into Execvision.

review_title: Execuvision review
description: Overall, it started out as a "this is a waste of my time" execercise. I felt like it was something that my boss had us doing just to waste our time. It turned out to be VERY helpful overall. I would absolutely recommend this product.
author: Ceddrick L.
pros: Most people want to be better without taking the time to use the tools that will make you better. Execuvision is one of those tools. At first i was resistant to it, but Execuvision turned out to be a great collaboration and coaching tool. We were able to share and discuss calls which lead to establishment of best practices as well as a sharing of techniques.
cons: Off hand there is nothing negative that comes to mind about Execuvision, at least not the software. Obviously, you have to set aside time to listen to your calls or the calls of others, but the benefits that are gained greatly outweigh any time spent going through the calls.

review_title: Best software for work
author: Phillip M.
pros: Easiness to use and ability to rate own calls, give feedback on myself
cons: I wish I could hear the other person!! (I know it is illegal, I just would love it)

review_title: ExecVision a solid choice for call coaching software
author: Austin J.
pros: ExecVision excels in 3 different areas: 1. Shareability with other ExecVision users. It is incredibly easy to send a call to other users. 2. Coaching is easy to see by being able to highlight certain portions of the call and adding comments to them. 3. Features/functions are really high level. Calls are easy to score, speed up/slow down, pause, comment on, share, etc.
cons: My one gripe with ExecVision is that calls don't pull up in the library instantly. They have to transfer over from Outreach and that can take up to 30 minutes. This delay often means I forget to share the calls I want to share and breaks up my day later. I wish that it could be quicker so that while I was thinking about that call I could request the coaching right then for the portion of the call I was thinking about.

review_title: Easy to use product which provides great value to both large and small teams.
description: The tech, sales and support teams are amazing. They are genuinely nice people who love what they do. This passion directly reflects on the quality of the product. The quality of the product and the support is phenomenal, and I cannot emphasize that enough.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It puts all the calls in one place which makes it easy for both the worker and the employee to review and coach on calls, The EV platform has a ton of features which directly result in productivity. Best of all the setup process is very easy, and the long term benefit you gain out of the platform is definitely worth what you would pay for the service. Over all it improves the productivity of the team. In my opinion, this product is first a behavioural coaching platform, and which naturally happens to be applicable to sales and so on.
cons: Not many cons that I know of. Most of the features are already at 100% . It is not a con, but the manager would still need to take out some time to review the calls. So as long as you have a good and proactive employee-employer relationship, and a pumped up team who are constantly looking to learn and deliver, and who have a manager who is equally responsive, then you are in for a real treat.

review_title: Good basic recording tool for organizations to learn from customers.
description: 1. Ease of use 2. Ability to highlight sections of a call 3. Ability to comment on section of a call 4. Ability to score a call 5. Ability to share portions or the entire call
author: Kevin P.
pros: ExecVision is an easy product to use. I like how it integrates into Salesforce. The ability to highlight a section of the call or video is helpful and sharing those sections with team members or back to customers is helpful in the process. It is a great tool to be able to go back and review important conversations during the sales process.
cons: I wish that you could label the call after it is recorded and uploaded. It just displays a call and you can't change it. Once you have a lot of calls in your library this becomes an issue. Also, the upload times can be slow, especially if it is a video like Zoom.

review_title: Very valuable to listen back on call recordings and request feedback from your team.
description: Building a knowledge base, getting feedback on call recordings, studying and using the recordings to become a better sales person.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Dive deeper into your call recordings, see how talk time can affect call outcome, tag call recordings and even request feedback on calls from your management team or colleagues. Great tool for sharing success calls and sharing best practices. Great training tool and knowledge library for new sales reps as well.
cons: It takes some time to get familiar with all the capabilities that ExecVision offers. We had a little difficulty getting it off and running with GoTo Meeting, but their support team is great.

review_title: It gets the job done!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It allows you to input notes at the intervals that you want.. What I like is that it records and uploads calls fairly quickly.
cons: It doesnt identify what part of the call is what. Ie. hook, pricing, etc. All calls do not sync up with the system.

review_title: No better way to review calls- a little difficult to use at times
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I can review my team's calls. There is little software out there that allows us to review calls- this is the best I've seen!
cons: Not very intuitive- ease of use is great but clunky and time to review calls is difficult. Need more functionality based upon needs of team- identifying areas on calls, scorecard customization.

review_title: Sometimes there are some quality issues but overall it works pretty well.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It shows you when someone is speaking so you can skip through static. I also like how many different filter options you have available.
cons: The delay in when the recording is posted seems to vary. Sometimes it may take 45 minutes and other times it seems to take hours. It would be nice to have it as quick as possible and also consistent.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Amazing idea and core functionality. Rough around the edges.
description: Despite some of my admin-based complaints about integrations, etc, this tool has been a huge help to our team. We've just brought on some senior sales leadership to our mostly young startup, and this has really helped let our sales leadership interface directly into what are reps are saying, when they say it, and how their delivery is. It has bridged a historical gap for us between management's theories and our team's actual practices.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like the ability to easily integrate with SF, and the core functionality - voice transcribing phone calls with the ability for coaches (or anyone, really) to leave specific comments at specific intervals and then pass back to the reps. Hall of Fame/Shame calls can be distributed among the team. And actually my favorite part is the repository of horrible calls our team keeps for laughs...folks who don't want to take sales calls says some pretty entertaining things...
cons: A lot of pieces of the integration feel a bit sloppy. Well, mostly the GoTo Meeting integration, but the Salesforce integration is essentially just a large query and there is no place for me as the admin to directly control the criteria of the query. It generally pulls data just fine, but as someone pretty familiar with queries, I would like some personal control without reaching out to support. The GTM integration, without going into specifics of how it works, has given us tremendous trouble for multiple reasons across multiple Users. They don't have a full custom interface for that, which may be coming, for now though, this part is half-baked.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: ExecVision Review
description: Overall, I believe that ExecVision is a useful tool to be using frequently in the training stages, and should continued to be used throughout your journey in sales. However, I believe that there should be more best practices iterated for manager-to-peer or peer-to-peer review sessions.
author: Kaitlyn L.
pros: -Liked having the option to review calls and understand where I made my mistakes and where I can improve for future calls. -Listen to call with Manager to understand where I can improve seems to be best practice for me rather than listen to a call, review the call, and then have to review and listen to it again with manager.
cons: -No SSO: stopped using the software for a long period of time because of this. -It takes time for the calls to be uploaded into ExecVision which can be frustrating because in the moment you want to figure out where your call went wrong before you proceed with more calls. -I never use the software to review previous feedback -The search tool is my worst enemy - I understand how to use it, but it's more complex than it should be. -Whenever I get a notification that someone marked a call in ExecVision, the link doesn't lead directly to the call & the call is hard for me to find. I would say that more "user friendly" notifications within the website (like a LinkedIn type notification page) would be beneficial.

review_title: Idea is there, but execution is not.
description: I am able to listen to my calls, review them for personal and professional growth. I love being able to listen to my calls for training purposes and be able to hear what I can do better. I love being able to listen to get better. I am also able to utilize it as a tool to confirm information.
author: Callan K.
pros: I love its functionality to listen to calls, score them, share them, store them, etc that all is great. I love the idea of percentages of who is talking in the conversation. It truly is great to listen to calls, have them in a software and be able to pause/score them. It is a great idea!!
cons: The information is inaccurate and disorganized. I can never find the calls that I want - not labeled correctly or easy to find. I really hate how I go to find a call and it is labeled wrong and I am unable to search it. Nothing is easily accessible or organized for the user to use easily. It takes FOREVER to find your call. I should be able to search it, be labeled correctly and see it there.

review_title: Love this tool
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Makes it really easy to break down calls by providing both parties with a visual representation of what happened during the call. As a trainer, this tool saves me time and allows me to be direct with my team.
cons: I have yet to find an issue with the software. It does what it is supposed to and is pretty easy to use.

review_title: Great Tool If Improvements Are made
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ease of use of being able to both record calls as well as take in files from G2M or Zoom. It is very easy to use the comments and library calls. This is a really good tool if a sales leader takes the time to actually use it for feedback
cons: Lots of bugs with the product. Upload times took days or would fail all together as well as the names would never save on calls or be periodically wiped so you could not search for anything. Salesforce integration was not really great as well.

review_title: Execvision was a Game - Changer for our ADM Team
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - I can share and comment on every call my team does. The ability to organize a framework and grade each call has been amazing at guiding us where we need the most coaching.
cons: - Hard report on how many calls each individual is actually reviewing. Would be cool to see more data.

review_title: ExecVision Review
description: It's very useful and helpful to have access to your calls when you need them.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's super handy being able to go back and listen to your calls. For training, review, and missing information.
cons: Most of the time is hard to locate a call that you are looking for. The filters hardly work also.

review_title: Not user friendly
author: Mohamad N.
pros: Being able to search for callers by name. It streamlines the process, makes it super easy to find your call
cons: User experience, the calls are not really prioritize. Seems hard to find a specific call. The phone app terrible

review_title: Decent Tool - Little heavy on price
description: ExecVision has been great for team members and myself to review calls, strong points and areas for improvement.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love the annotation and UI for call reviews.
cons: It has been a bit buggy, kicking me out quite a bit lately when trying to review calls.
